items.title: Flat base change (Stacks 02KH)
items.summary:
**Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **Beck–Chevalley heart of 02KG closed**: `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image member node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), and the **per-$\sigma$ RHS tilde leaf** `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` are sorry-free and axiom-clean. Pullback along an **arbitrary** scheme morphism now preserves quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, Stacks 01BG). Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **4 leaf sorries**: the `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` affine-gap heart (`lem:coverinter_rhs_tensor_rewrite`), the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields, and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2**.

comments.author: ground
comments.body:
**Beck–Chevalley member node for the open-immersion base-change square is now closed (T2, run 0003).**

The hard heart of the termwise affine base change (Stacks 02KG) — that for an affine open $V_0\hookrightarrow X$ ($X$ separated) and quasi-coherent $\mathcal{F}$, the natural map
$$g'^{*}\big((V_0)_{*}\,(V_0)^{*}\mathcal{F}\big)\ \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\ (V_0')_{*}\,(V_0')^{*}(g'^{*}\mathcal{F})$$
is an isomorphism — is proved. Route: the mate factors so that $\mathrm{IsIso}\,\mathrm{bareBC}$ collapses to essential-image membership of $p'_{*}$, which is cover-local; the member node is discharged over each affine $W_j$ by the new `AffinePushPullEssImage.lean` via the abstract ring-pushout square. Consequently `openImmersion_beckChevalley` and `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma` are sorry-free and axiom-clean (`propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound`); the full AJC `lake build` is green (8640 jobs).

**Evidence:** 5 real `sorry` tokens remain in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` (down from 7), all blueprinted leaves toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`; all 7 `\lean`-pinned decls verified present and the 3 relevant blueprint nodes carry `\leanok`. items.title: Jacobian = Albanese (top goal)
items.summary:
**North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

items.title: Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3)
items.summary:
**$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- `derivationEquivCotangentDual`: $\mathrm{Der}_k(R,\kappa)\cong\Hom_R(\mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2,\kappa)$ via the $R=k\oplus\mathfrak m$ splitting (k-rational point hypothesis).
- `localDualNumberHomEquivDerivation` / `...CotangentSpaceDual`: dual-number points $\to$ derivations $\to (\mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2)^\vee$.

Wired into `thm:pic0_tangent_space_iso` proof `\uses` (6 new `\leanok` nodes in `Picard_Pic0AbelianVariety.tex`).

v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.

Recipes: closing GeometricallyReduced / GeometricallyIrreducible scaffold sorries (v4.31 Mathlib has the full API)

Mathlib v4.31 ships `AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/{Basic,Reduced,Irreducible}.lean` — these scaffold instances are now provable, not gaps. Proven patterns (see `Points.lean` `gm_geometricallyReduced`, `ChartIso.lean` `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`, both axiom-clean):

- **Affine case** (`Spec A ⟶ Spec k̄`): `rw [geometricallyReduced_iff / geometricallyIrreducible_iff]`, intro the `IsPullback`, `obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y; algebraize [φ.hom]`, then `h.isoPullback ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso k̄ A K` reduces to `IsReduced/IsDomain (A ⊗[k̄] K)`. Needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (the `(Gm k̄).hom`-to-`Spec.map` defeq).
- **Ring step for localizations**: `IsReduced (Localization.Away t ⊗[k̄] K)` via `Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange k̄ P P _ K` (`≅ Loc ⊗[P] (P ⊗[k̄] K)`), instance `IsLocalization.tensor`, and `isReduced_localizationPreserves` (apply with `by exact` + explicit S — beta-reduction quirk). `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm` give `P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial σ K`.
- **Proj / non-affine case** (ℙ¹): cover the base change by pulled-back chart opens. Per chart: `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero_assoc` + `← Spec.map_comp` + `change` collapses `awayι ≫ hom` to `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`, then the affine recipe. Covering: `Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top` with the (now named) `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span`. Intersection: any preimage of the generic point `⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, …⟩` under `pullback.fst` (surjective — `Surjective` is `IsStableUnderBaseChange`, `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst`). Assemble with the new `irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens` (ChartIso).
- **Traps**: inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, ring-level `IsReduced` must be `_root_.IsReduced`; `⊗[k̄]` notation needs `open scoped TensorProduct in` BEFORE the docstring; avoid `ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _` (leaves an unsynthesizable eta-expanded `IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms` goal — use `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (iso).hom` or `.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff` instead); the affine cover's `I₀`/`𝒰.f i` are semireducibly opaque — work with `Proj.awayι (X i)` + `fin_cases` normalization (`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux` idiom) rather than cover indices.

**Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.

**Recipe: Stacks 00TT at ARBITRARY primes over a perfect field, Serre-free (supersedes the "00OF is the remaining genuine gap / do not attempt" guidance — 00OF is no longer needed).**

Proven in `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` (axiom-clean, v4.31 Mathlib suffices). Structure, reusable for similar dimension-theory builds:

- **Lemma A (trdeg–height)**: for prime `P ⊆ k[x_ι]`, `∃ d ≤ trdeg k (k[x_ι]⧸P)` with `#ι ≤ ht P + d`. Induction via `Finite.induction_empty_option`; one-variable step uses Stacks 00ON `Ideal.height_eq_height_add_of_liesOver_of_hasGoingDown` (+ `Algebra.HasGoingDown.of_flat`, `Module.Flat.of_free`) and splits on the fiber prime in `(R⧸p)[X]` (`Ideal.polynomialQuotientEquivQuotientPolynomial`); the `∃ d ≤`-witness form avoids ALL fraction fields and exact-trdeg bookkeeping. Key trdeg API: `trdeg_add_le`, `trdeg_le_of_injective`, `Polynomial.trdeg_of_isDomain` (all root-namespace!).
- **Lemma C**: `rank Ω[K⁄k] = trdeg k K` for `EssFiniteType` field ext of perfect `k`: `exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField` (returns a `Finset`!) + `Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable` + `tensorKaehlerEquivOfFormallyEtale` + `AlgebraicIndependent.aevalEquivField` + `Basis.ofIsLocalizedModule` over `mvPolynomialBasis`.
- **Lemma D**: `dim_κ m/m² + dim_κ Ω[κ⁄R] = n` generalising iter-199 by dropping `Subsingleton Ω[κ/R]`: same `iff_split_injection` retraction + `exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange` + `mapBaseChange_surjective` + rank–nullity. `FormallySmooth k κ` is FREE from the new low-priority instance `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField [EssFiniteType k κ]`.
- Assembly: `finrank Cot ≤ ht q` by ℕ∞ cancellation (`ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`, pass `k :=` explicitly), then `IsRegularLocalRing.of_finrank_cotangentSpace_le_ringKrullDim`.

**Traps**: (1) NEVER bridge AlgEquiv/RingEquiv coercion paths with `Iff.rfl`/`rfl` at FUNCTION level (whnf timeout on `renameEquiv`); state helpers with pointwise membership hypotheses (`∀ a, a ∈ P₀ ↔ g a ∈ P`) instead. (2) `have`-bound equivs are opaque — use `set ... with` when a later proof must compute through the definition. (3) In `of_equiv` induction branches don't `rename_i` (grabs instances); avoid naming the types. (4) Multi-step `rw` chains on algebraMap-towers can close the goal mid-list and error — split into per-equation `have`s. (5) `Basis` is now `Module.Basis` (open Module).

**leandag audit convention: the DAG engine registers only STATEMENT-level `\uses`; proof-level `\uses` are ignored.**

**Recipe: ValuativeCommSq for rational-map extension (Milne 3.1 codim-≥2 CLOSED); over-k̄ hypothesis is required.**

`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (Albanese `CodimOneExtension.lean`) is proved axiom-clean — Mathlib v4.31 has ALL the plumbing; do not hand-roll rational-map/valuative machinery. Reusable recipe:

- **Pipeline**: `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` + Mathlib `IsFractionRing (stalk z) K(X)` (integral schemes) + DVR stalk ⇒ `ValuativeCommSq Y.hom`; extract the lift from `IsProper.eq_valuativeCriterion` (`rw at hP; exact hP.1.1.1`); spread out with `PartialMap.ofFromSpecStalk X.hom Y.hom L hfacr` (`[X.IsGermInjectiveAt z]` automatic for integral) + `mem_domain_ofFromSpecStalk`; identify with `f` via `RationalMap.eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq` + a small glue lemma `fromFunctionField = Spec.map (stalkSpecializes gen⤳z) ≫ fromSpecStalkOfMem` (prove by `cancel_mono g.domain.ι` + `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` + `SpecMap_stalkSpecializes_fromSpecStalk`).
- `CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap (stalk z) functionField) = stalkSpecializes ((genericPoint_spec X).specializes trivial)` is **rfl** (`stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra` def) — exploit by defeq (`exact`), do NOT `rw` (higher-order pattern fails).
- **Structure-literal trap**: inside a `ValuativeCommSq { ... }` literal the stalk term gets unfolded past `Scheme.presheaf`, so keyed instances (`IsDomain (stalk z)`, `Field functionField`, `IsFractionRing`, `stalkFunctionFieldAlgebra`) STOP firing. Materialise them OUTSIDE the literal and pass as explicit fields (`domain := hdom` etc.).
- **Field-poisoning trap**: bind those facts with plain `have`/`let`, NEVER `haveI/letI` — an opaque local `Field functionField` fvar shadows the CommRing path and makes `Algebra (stalk z) K(X)` unsynthesizable. Also needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (IsField.toField / CommRingCat.of-eta walls).
- **Over-k̄ is mathematically necessary**: the valuative square's base compatibility needs `hf : f.compHom Y.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap` (Milne's maps are k̄-maps). The whole Thm-3.2 chain now threads this hypothesis; future consumers must supply it (for maps built from k̄-morphisms it follows via `Hom.toRationalMap`/`compHom_toRationalMap`). Push it to the function field via `exists_rep` + `compHom_toRationalMap` + simp with `fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom/toPartialMap`.
- Coheight conventions: scheme points carry `specializationPreorder` (Mathlib instance, `x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`); coheight 0 ⇔ `IsMax` (`Order.coheight_eq_zero`) ⇒ generic point via `Specializes.antisymm ...|>.eq`; `ENat.lt_two_iff` for the ≤1 split.
- Remaining CodimOneExtension gaps are NOT valuative: Stacks 0AVF (depth-≥2 H¹, Milne 3.1 Step 2) and Milne 3.3 difference-map machinery.

**`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.

local_inbox.body:
pullback_preservesFiniteLimits: prove via flat extendScalars, NOT adjointness

`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (`CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:193`, instance, `[Flat g]`) is a **true** statement but a genuine open `sorry` — do not attempt a cheap adjointness proof.

- The statement is sound: module pullback $g^*$ = inverse image $g^{-1}$ (exact) followed by $-\otimes_{\mathcal O_S}\mathcal O_{S'}$, which is left-exact **because** `[Flat g]`. Finite limits are preserved.
- The trap: `g^*` is a **left adjoint**, so it preserves colimits / is right-exact — that gives you nothing for finite *limits*. "Left adjoint $\Rightarrow$ left-exact" is backwards. The flatness hypothesis is doing all the work and must be used.
- Intended route (per the leaf's own docstring): transfer along `SheafOfModules.pullbackIso`, discharge `forget`/`sheafification` (already limit-preserving in Mathlib), reduce to `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` stalkwise. Reference Stacks 02KH.
- Note it is a global `instance` carrying a sorry; `pullback_preservesHomology` (line 197) derives from it by `inferInstance`, so the whole flat-homology chain rests on this node.

v4.31 recipes round 3 (MR0555258 migration): convert descends deeper, Type-hom funext wall, structInst binder-field parse quirk

New patterns from migrating `MR0555258/Basic.lean` v4.30→v4.31 (all verified green, complement the existing v4.31 memories):

- **`convert h using N` descends one level deeper in v4.31**: proofs shaped `convert h using 4; exact congrArg f e` break with a type-mismatch whose 'expected' is the *argument-level* equality. Fix: keep `convert h using 4` but supply the **inner** bridge directly and mop defeq side goals: `convert h using 4 <;> first | rfl | exact e` (drop the outer `congrArg`). Extra side goals are object/category equalities closed by `rfl`.
- **Type-category hom equality: `funext`/`apply funext` does NOT unify** with `f ≫ g = h ≫ i` even under `respectTransparency false`. Mathlib's own idiom (see `FullyFaithful.homNatIso`): `fun f => by ext g; exact hF.map_injective (by simp)`. If simp stalls on `hG.homEquiv.symm` (v4.31 displays via `ConcreteCategory.hom ... .toFun`), run `simp` first, then `show`-restate with `hG.preimage` (defeq to `homEquiv.symm`), then `simp` (fires `map_preimage`).
- **StructInst binder-style field + comma**: `{ app V := e, naturality := ... }` fails to parse ('unexpected identifier; expected }'). Use lambda + newline-separated fields: `{ app := fun V => e\n  naturality := ... }`.
- **`Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_naturality` exists in v4.31 Mathlib** — exact match for presheaf-map-vs-appIso.inv naturality squares of open immersions; no hand proof needed.
- **`lake update` with a dep pinned to `main` (doc-gen4) silently bumps `lean-toolchain`** (to v4.32.0-rc1 here) and then the mathlib cache refuses to fetch. Pin doc-gen4 to `0bc516c1b9db` (the AJC rev) and rewrite `lean-toolchain` back before re-running.
